findings.title: Anyone-Can-Spend Function
findings.description: One or more contract functions contain no require() statements. In CashScript, every spending condition must be enforced with require(). A function with no require() can be satisfied by any transaction input.
findings.exploit_explanation: An attacker constructs a transaction spending the contract UTXO by calling the vulnerable function with arbitrary (or empty) arguments. Because there are no script assertions, BCHN's script engine always returns true and accepts the spend. The attacker receives all funds locked at the contract address.
findings.remediation: Add at least one require() statement to every function. For ownership checks use require(checkSig(sig, ownerPk)). For data integrity use require(hash256(data) == expectedHash). Never leave a function body empty or with only comments.

executive_summary: Automated executive summary unavailable. Overall risk: Critical. Total findings: 1 (Critical: 1, High: 0, Medium: 0, Low: 0). 1 finding(s) were verified with a working PoC exploit in regtest.
